title: Cyclea hypoglauca

The plant belongs to the genus Cyclea, which is a part of the Annonaceae family, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. Cyclea hypoglauca is classified within the family Annonaceae, which consists of over 2,000 species of flowering plants. The classification of Cyclea hypoglauca is further supported by its unique morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.

intro: Cyclea hypoglauca, also known as Indian snail vine or Cyclea, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, potentially benefiting the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
